title: Vibrant Brine-Dry Mexican Corn Elote
description: Spicy, crumbly, monsoon Mexican dish featuring corn flour, brine-dry, low-fat-friendly, form=elote, vessel=slate-tile.
instructions: Grind the ancho chili peppers into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle.
In a large bowl, mix together the corn flour, water, lime juice, salt, ancho chili powder, cumin, and cayenne pepper.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it is smooth and pliable.
Divide the dough into 8 equal pieces and shape each piece into a ball.
Heat the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the corn kernels to the skillet and cook for 5 minutes, stirring frequently.
Add the dough balls to the skillet and cook for an additional 5 minutes, until the dough is crispy and golden brown.
Serve the elote hot, garnished with additional lime wedges and chili powder if desired.
